title: A new radical left is shaking up the Democratic party
subhed: A new generation of candidates, critical of elites, social injustice and US support for Israel, is making its mark in the Democratic primaries ahead of November's midterm elections.

title: Choice of presidential election dates raises questions
subhed: The first round of the next presidential election is set for April 18, 2027, and the runoff on May 2, just one day after Labor Day, a highly symbolic date in France.

title: Paris Opera house renovations extended by three years
subhed: The iconic and beloved Paris Opera will close the Palais Garnier site from 2027-2032, three years longer than initially planned due to lead contamination.

title: How can we prepare together for the next heatwave?
subhed: France will experience many more heatwaves and some will be even more severe than the last one, which was historic. To cope, we need to get organized collectively – without giving up the fight to reduce our greenhouse gas emissions, Le Monde's climate expert writes.

title: 'We should not accept that heatwaves cause thousands of deaths. That is not normal'
subhed: In an interview with Le Monde, Julien Dossier, founder of the environmental consulting firm Quattrolibri, offers an initial political assessment of how France responded to the most intense heatwave ever recorded in the country.

title: 250 years of American independence: In 1976, a bicentennial celebrated with patriotism despite divisions
subhed: 'America 250' (10/13). In 1976, the United States turned a page on one of the most turbulent chapters in its history. After the Vietnam War and the resignation of Richard Nixon, the bicentennial offered Americans a moment of relief. Minority rights, social justice – new debates continued to stir society, notably led by the economist Jeremy Rifkin and the 'red, white and blue' left.
